task.title: Phase 5 Capstone Exercise: End-to-End DITA Project
task.shortdesc: Bring together all skills from Phases 1-4 in a comprehensive capstone project. Technical writers focus on content strategy and multi-variant publishing. Software engineers focus on automation, CI/CD, and plugin polish.

ul.li: Completed all previous phase exercises
Read Part 9 (AI Integration) and Part 10 (Benefits and Impact) topics
Working TaskFlow documentation project with plugin

context.p: This capstone combines everything into a realistic documentation project. You will expand your TaskFlow docs into a production-quality deliverable with multiple audiences, automated builds, and quality checks.
Choose the track matching your role, or complete both for full mastery.

step.cmd: [Writer Track] Expand the content library to 8+ topics.

info.p: Add these new topics to your TaskFlow project:

ol.li: Concept: "TaskFlow Architecture" — explain the component architecture
Task: "Importing and Exporting Data" — data management workflow
Reference: "Configuration File Reference" — table of all config options

ul.li: Use keyrefs for all product names
Use conref for repeated content (system requirements, support info)
Add audience profiling (end-user, admin, developer) where appropriate
Add metadata with keywords and index terms to every topic

step.cmd: [Writer Track] Create a bookmap with proper front matter.

info.p: Convert your flat map to a <bookmap> with:

ul.li: <booktitle> with main and alternative titles
<bookmeta> with author and copyright info
<frontmatter> with a preface topic
<chapter> elements grouping related topics
<backmatter> with a glossary

step.cmd: [Writer Track] Build 3 audience variants and compare outputs.

info.p: Create three DITAVAL files and build each:

ol.li: End-user guide: excludes admin and developer content
Admin guide: includes admin, excludes developer
Developer guide: includes developer, excludes admin

info.p: Create a comparison document noting which topics/sections appear in each variant. This demonstrates the ROI of single-source publishing.

step.cmd: [Engineer Track] Create a build script for all variants.

info.p: Create build.sh that builds all variants in sequence:
info.codeblock:
#!/bin/bash set -e
DITAMAP="taskflow.ditamap" DITA_CMD="dita"
echo "Building TaskFlow Documentation..."
# Clean output rm -rf out/
# End-user HTML5 echo "==> Building end-user HTML5..." $DITA_CMD -i "$DITAMAP" -f taskflow-html5 \ -o out/html5-enduser \ --filter=filters/end-user.ditaval
# Admin HTML5 echo "==> Building admin HTML5..." $DITA_CMD -i "$DITAMAP" -f taskflow-html5 \ -o out/html5-admin \ --filter=filters/admin.ditaval
# PDF (unfiltered) echo "==> Building PDF..." $DITA_CMD -i "$DITAMAP" -f pdf \ -o out/pdf
echo "Done! Outputs in out/"

step.cmd: [Engineer Track] Create a GitHub Actions workflow for CI/CD.

info.codeblock:
name: Build Documentation on: push: branches: [main] paths: ['taskflow-docs/**'] pull_request: paths: ['taskflow-docs/**']
jobs: build: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4
- name: Setup Java uses: actions/setup-java@v4 with: java-version: '17' distribution: 'temurin'
- name: Install DITA-OT run: | curl -sL https://github.com/dita-ot/dita-ot/releases/download/4.2.3/dita-ot-4.2.3.zip -o dita-ot.zip unzip -q dita-ot.zip echo "$PWD/dita-ot-4.2.3/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
- name: Install custom plugin run: | cp -r com.taskflow.html5 dita-ot-4.2.3/plugins/ dita --install
- name: Build all variants run: bash build.sh
- name: Upload artifacts uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 with: name: taskflow-docs path: out/
info.note: This CI pipeline ensures every push builds the docs. Failed builds block merges — treating docs like code with the same quality gates.

step.cmd: [Engineer Track] Dockerize the build for reproducibility.

info.codeblock:
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine
# Install DITA-OT ARG DITA_OT_VERSION=4.2.3 RUN wget -q "https://github.com/dita-ot/dita-ot/releases/download/${DITA_OT_VERSION}/dita-ot-${DITA_OT_VERSION}.zip" \ && unzip -q "dita-ot-${DITA_OT_VERSION}.zip" \ && rm "dita-ot-${DITA_OT_VERSION}.zip"
ENV DITA_HOME=/dita-ot-${DITA_OT_VERSION} ENV PATH="${DITA_HOME}/bin:${PATH}"
# Install custom plugin COPY com.taskflow.html5/ ${DITA_HOME}/plugins/com.taskflow.html5/ RUN dita --install
WORKDIR /docs ENTRYPOINT ["dita"]
docker build -t taskflow-dita . docker run --rm -v "$PWD/taskflow-docs:/docs" \ taskflow-dita -i taskflow.ditamap -f taskflow-html5 -o /docs/out

step.cmd: [Both] Prepare your content for AI retrieval (Part 9 application).

info.p: Apply the concepts from Part 9 (AI Integration) to your TaskFlow project:

ol.li: Add id attributes to every <section> — these become chunk boundaries for RAG ingestion
Rephrase key section titles as questions (matching how users ask chatbots) — e.g. "How do I configure TaskFlow?" instead of "Configuration"
Ensure every topic has complete prolog metadata: <audience>, <category>, <keywords>

Write a one-page architecture brief describing how a RAG pipeline would ingest your DITA content — reference the 4-stage pipeline (ingest → retrieve → rerank → generate) from ch11-rag-pipeline-architecture.dita

info.note: Compare your metadata-enriched topics with the samples/before/ and samples/after/ files to verify you are following AI-ready patterns.

step.cmd: [Both] Perform a final quality review of your complete project.

ul.li: ☐ All topics build without errors or warnings
☐ All keyrefs resolve (no "???" in output)
☐ All conrefs pull content correctly
☐ DITAVAL filtering produces distinct variants
☐ Every topic has metadata (keywords, audience)
☐ Navigation structure is logical and complete
☐ HTML5 output displays branding (styled notes, code blocks)
☐ PDF output generates successfully

step.cmd: [Both] Write a project summary documenting your architecture decisions.

ul.li: Project structure and topic inventory
Reuse strategy (what is conref'd, what uses keys)
Audience variants and how filtering works
Build instructions (manual and automated)
What you would improve or add next

info.p: This README serves as documentation for anyone maintaining the project after you.

p.b: Writer track — you should now be able to:

ul.li: ✓ Architect a multi-topic DITA project with bookmap structure
✓ Implement content reuse at scale (conref, keyref)
✓ Produce filtered variants for different audiences
✓ Enrich content with metadata for findability
✓ Evaluate documentation quality and completeness

p.b: Engineer track — you should now be able to:

ul.li: ✓ Build and maintain DITA-OT plugins for branded output
✓ Automate builds with shell scripts
✓ Set up CI/CD pipelines for documentation
✓ Containerize DITA-OT builds with Docker
✓ Apply production hardening practices

result.note: Congratulations! You have completed the full DITA learning path. Continue your learning by exploring the advanced topics in Parts 9 and 10: AI pipeline integration (RAG, chunking, embeddings) and measuring documentation impact (ROI, utilization metrics).
